private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Deterrence is a Seed-stage defense and security company that has raised $10M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public and 8 private—operating in the Defense & Security sector, including Series A-stage peers such as Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M), as well as Seed-stage reference Tenna Systems ($60M).

Two valuation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method uses the $10M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage step-ups, yielding $25M. The Peer-Median method benchmarks against five same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $150M (range: $60M–$500M). This reflects the significant valuation spread within early-stage defense tech, driven by variation in funding round sizes and investor appetite.

Triangulating these two approaches produces a range of $21M to $172M, with a median of $88M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around where Deterrence sits relative to its peer set—particularly whether the company's technology, market traction, or team composition merits positioning closer to lower Seed benchmarks like Tenna Systems or higher Series A comparables. Additional due diligence on customer validation, IP defensibility, and capital efficiency would narrow this range materially.
